goldUseNotes: Approved only as a manual calibration artifact after GPT/Gemini/Opus review. It is a positive C4 example because LEANING-TRUE 61/54 is inside the primary route, but it is not Captain-approved gold. The dossier's current top-line sentence-proportionality assertion is not addressed by this report, so C1/C3 remain partial and diagnostic.

acceptedAdjustments: Mark TC_SENTENCE_PROPORTIONALITY_FAIRNESS and RA_SENTENCE_PROPORTIONALITY_FAIRNESS_CAVEATED_TRUE_SIDE as not addressed.
Remove the proposed C3 row for RA_SENTENCE.
Keep benchmarkPass as a mechanical C4 route pass only.
Record that MIXED versus true-side out-of-band C3 needs a future explicit enum.
reviewerConvergence.dossierFollowUp: Reviewers flagged that using sentence proportionality as bolsonaro-en topLineAssertionIds may be too narrow for the Captain wording, which asks about proceedings and verdicts. Treat this as a dossier-v0.2 review item, not a blocker for this calibration-only artifact.

reportSnapshot.inputValue: Did the legal proceedings against Jair Bolsonaro comply with Brazilian law, and did the proceedings and the verdicts meet international standards for a fair trial?
reportSnapshot.canonicalInputValue: Did the legal proceedings against Jair Bolsonaro comply with Brazilian law, and did the proceedings and the verdicts meet international standards for a fair trial?

reportAtomicClaims.statement: The legal proceedings against Jair Bolsonaro complied with Brazilian law.
The proceedings against Jair Bolsonaro met international standards for a fair trial.
The verdicts against Jair Bolsonaro met international standards for a fair trial.

claimMappings.notes: The broad domestic-law claim visibly covers the TSE background, STF/AP2668 legal basis, and domestic safeguards in the verdict reasoning, but it merges three dossier truth conditions into one AtomicClaim.
Clean mapping to the proceedings-level international fair-trial condition, albeit with a more cautious MIXED C3 verdict than the dossier reference.
The report assesses verdict fair-trial standards, but does not cleanly assess broad legal supportability of the TSE and STF verdicts as the reference assertion frames it.

truthConditionMappings.notes: TSE proceeding is mentioned in the AC_01 reasoning, but not extracted as a separate AtomicClaim.
STF/AP2668 legal basis is directly visible in AC_01 reasoning but merged into broad domestic-law compliance.
Domestic safeguards are directly visible in AC_01 reasoning but merged with legal-basis material.
Verdict legality/supportability is only partially visible through a verdict-level fair-trial claim.
Reviewer-confirmed correction: the report does not assess sentence proportionality or sentence fairness, even though this is the current dossier top-line assertion.

assertionCoverage.notes: The TSE proceeding is present in reasoning but not separately extracted, and dissent treatment is not independently developed.
The report directly discusses STF trial, statutory basis, appellate procedure, final appeals, res judicata, and enforcement.
The report addresses verdict fair-trial standards but not the full legal-supportability assertion for both TSE and STF verdicts.
The report does not separately assess sentence proportionality or fairness of the sentence.

c1.notes: Positive C4 calibration artifact with partial C1/C3 fit. The report lands in the expected true-side aggregate band and has a useful three-claim shape, but it does not cover the dossier's sentence-proportionality top-line assertion. Reviewers flagged this as both an artifact correction and a possible bolsonaro-en dossier-v0.2 design issue.

assertionVerdictFits.notes: AC_01 is MOSTLY-TRUE 74/65. Truth and label fit the TSE reference band, but confidence is below the 70-90 band and the TSE subtopic is merged into a broad domestic-law claim.
AC_01 is MOSTLY-TRUE 74/65 against a TRUE 86-100 / 75-95 reference assertion. This is true-side but below the dossier's legal-basis strength.
AC_01 MOSTLY-TRUE 74/65 fits the domestic-safeguards reference band.

AC_02 MIXED 50/45 is below the reference true-side 58-85 band but adjacent to LEANING-TRUE and confidence is inside the 45-75 band. The report is more cautious than the reference, not direction-flipped.

AC_03 MIXED 52/52 is materially below the MOSTLY-TRUE 72-85 / 55-75 reference band and only partially maps to verdict legal supportability. The v0.3 enum lacks an explicit neutral-out-of-band label, so this is encoded as the nearest non-flip out-of-band value and flagged in c3.reviewFlags.

c4Note.notes: The report passes the primary aggregate C4 route mechanically: LEANING-TRUE 61/54 is inside the expected labels, truth band, and confidence band. This does not imply full C1/C3 alignment because the current dossier top-line assertion on sentence proportionality is not addressed.
